pattern: Check all four 2x2 sub-squares; a single color change is possible when at least one 2x2 block has 3 or 4 cells of the same color.
hints: There are exactly four 2x2 sub-squares in a 3x3 grid; enumerate each by its top-left corner.
For each 2x2 block, count how many cells are 'B' and how many are 'W'.
You can flip at most one cell, so the block is satisfiable when it already has 3 or 4 cells of the same color.

edge_inputs.description: almost checkerboard — one block has 3 of same
